prompt: Photorealistic 3D render of a perfect geometric cube with SHARP hard edges and FLAT faces, NO rounded corners, NO bevels, rendered in Blender Cycles with physically-based materials. CRITICAL: edges must be razor sharp, perfectly straight, 90-degree angles at every corner. This is a mathematical cube, not a rounded squircle. PERSPECTIVE: the cube is rotated 35 degrees on the Y axis and 25 degrees on the X axis, viewed from a 3/4 camera position showing three faces clearly with strong perspective depth. MATERIAL: smooth glossy plastic-like surface in electric cyan #00E3FF, uniform across all faces. Subtle subsurface scattering giving it a slight translucent glow, plus sharp specular highlights where the light hits directly. The top face is slightly brighter, the right face mid-tone, the left face darker — classic cube shading. WIREFRAME OVERLAY: a precise thin white wireframe mesh (#FFFFFF at 70% opacity) on each face, evenly spaced grid lines forming a 6x6 grid on each visible face. Lines follow the flat surface perfectly with proper perspective foreshortening. LIGHTING: three-point lighting — strong key light from upper-left, fill light from right, rim light from behind creating edge highlights on the sharp corners. BACKGROUND: pure black #000000, completely solid black, no gradient, no glow, just absolute darkness. Subtle contact shadow beneath. COMPOSITION: cube centered with generous negative space. Crisp edges, high anti-aliasing, no noise, no blur. Must read as a hard-edged geometric solid.
